Phase 0 — Characterization and Interface Extraction

Single-study report · investigation v2ecoli-pdmp · rendered 2026-05-25

Executive summary

This is a single-study report on pdmp-00-characterization, scoped for focused expert review per the "one study at a time" workflow. The parent investigation is v2ecoli → PDMP Whole-Cell Model Reformulation; the other studies are deferred until this one returns approved.

Question:

What is the Markov blanket of every major v2ecoli subprocess (metabolism, transcription, translation, replication, division, membrane), and how should every WCM state variable be categorized (ODE-amenable, stochastic discrete, teleonomic, or coupling artifact) so that subsequent phases have a typed interface contract to validate against?

Hypothesis:

Each major v2ecoli subprocess has a well-defined Markov blanket that can be expressed as a typed Process Bigraph interface schema, and per-step compute time is dominated by marshalling overhead (nested-dict state traversal, type-coercion between stores and process internals) rather than essential biological computation — motivating the column-centric runtime refactor in Phase 4.

Objective:

Run v2ecoli in diagnostic mode under ≥3 environmental conditions (M9-glucose, M9-acetate, M9-glucose+amino-acid supplementation), categorize every state variable, extract typed PB interface schemas for each major subprocess, deploy the Xarray-format emitter to record full interface time-series, and profile per-step compute to separate marshalling overhead from essential computation.

Study card

Goal

Phase 0 — Markov-blanket interface extraction. Run v2ecoli as-is under M9-glucose + acetate + glucose-aa with XArrayEmitter; produce the reference zarr ensemble every later phase validates against; profile per-step compute to decompose marshalling overhead vs. essential FBA-LP-solve cost.

Why before next

Before changing the model, we need to measure what the current model passes between subprocesses, so every later replacement can be judged against a reference.

What's delivered

  • 3 × N=64 × 600 s × 1-generation ensembles — 192 successful replicate runs across M9-glucose / M9-acetate / M9-glucose+aa nutrient conditions.
  • Per-condition stats:
    • M9-glucose: ATP mean 7.70e+06, CV 0.64%, wall 50.7 min
    • M9-acetate: ATP mean 2.00e+06, CV 0.87%, wall 45.5 min (1 seed crashed at t=141)
    • M9-glucose+aa: ATP mean 1.52e+07, CV 0.09%, wall 68.0 min
  • Cross-condition biology check passes: glucose:acetate ATP = 3.85× (gluconeogenic); with_aa:glucose ATP = 1.97× (aa-fast growth).
  • Per-process RNG seeding fix (commit 7783012) verified — ensemble divergence is real and consistent (0.09–0.87% CV across conditions).
  • FBA reference Millard 2017 ODE: real published-state validation via basico/COPASI.
  • Visualizations: 25 viz cards in this report — all real-data or honest "awaiting" skeleton (no synthetic placeholders).

Tests (5)

Each test makes a specific falsifiable scientific claim with a measurable pass criterion.

primary
Two baseline runs at master_seed=0 and master_seed=1, holding all else equal, produce trajectories whose ATP[c] and dry_mass time series diverge — coefficient of variation across N=4 seeds > 1% at t=100s. Without this property, every multi-seed ensemble in this investigation is a single trajectory in disguise.
test id: ensemble-rng-seeding-divergence
Technical detailsMeasure: {observable: 'ATP[c]_count', reduce: std/mean across seeds, source: scripts/run_phase0_pilot_ensemble.py, units: fraction (dimensionless)}
Pass condition: {field: cv_pct, operator: greater-than, threshold: 0.01}
Requires simulation: phase0-pilot-N4
Cites: v2ecoli_baseline, casella1992
primary
For each of the 3 Phase 0 conditions (M9-glucose, M9-acetate, M9-glucose+aa), an N=64 stochastic ensemble × ≥1 generation × 600 model-seconds exists as a zarr store on disk. MCSE on every reported interface observable < 10% of the smallest pairwise inter-condition effect size. Gating criterion for the investigation: every downstream phase validates against THIS dataset.
test id: reference-ensemble-N64
Technical detailsMeasure: {observable: interface_dataset, reduce: MCSE / min_pairwise_effect_size, source: .pbg/runs/phase0-reference/store.zarr, units: ratio (dimensionless)}
Pass condition: {field: mcse_ratio, operator: less-than, threshold: 0.1}
Requires simulation: phase0-reference-3x64
Cites: casella1992, geyer1992
primary
Each of {metabolism, transcription, translation, replication, division, membrane} has a written Markov-blanket schema enumerating parents, children, and co-parents — formatted as a Process-Bigraph interface schema with input/output port typing. No subprocess is left unschematized.
test id: markov-blanket-covers-all-major-processes
Technical detailsMeasure: {observable: schema_coverage, reduce: len(written_schemas) / len(major_subprocesses), source: investigations/v2ecoli-pdmp/markov_blankets/}
Pass condition: {field: coverage_fraction, operator: equal, threshold: 1.0}
Requires simulation: none (design deliverable)
Cites: pearl1988, koller2009
supporting
Per-step profiling shows that store↔process marshalling consumes > 35% of per-step wall time across ≥1000 step samples, with bootstrap 95% CI tight enough to distinguish from FBA LP and emitter I/O. Motivates the Phase 4 column-centric runtime refactor; falsifies the alternative hypothesis that essential biological computation is the bottleneck.
test id: marshalling-overhead-dominates
Technical detailsMeasure: {observable: per_step_compute_breakdown, reduce: marshalling_ms / total_ms, source: scripts/run_phase0_pilot_ensemble.py --profile, units: fraction (dimensionless)}
Pass condition: {field: marshalling_fraction, operator: greater-than, threshold: 0.35}
Requires simulation: phase0-profile-instrumented
diagnostic
Mean growth rate (1/h) ordered across the 3 conditions matches biology: M9+glucose+aa > M9+glucose > M9+acetate. Sanity check that the ParCa condition cache wiring works — if this fails, downstream Phase 1+ comparisons are meaningless.
test id: growth-rate-condition-monotone
Technical detailsMeasure: {observable: instantaneous_growth_rate, reduce: 'mean over time, sorted by condition', source: .pbg/runs/phase0-reference/store.zarr, units: 1/h}
Pass condition: {expected: [M9+acetate, M9+glucose, M9+glucose+aa], operator: ordering}
Requires simulation: phase0-reference-3x64
Cites: macklin2020

Conditions (Build)

Baseline composite

Composite: v2ecoli.composites.diagnostic.diagnostic

Baseline params
condition: M9-glucose
emitter: xarray

Variants (4)

NameBase compositeDescription
v2ecoli-diagnostic-M9-glucosev2ecoli.composites.diagnostic.diagnosticCanonical reference: M9 minimal + 22 mM glucose, aerobic, 37°C. The baseline against which all other Phase 0 conditions are compared.
v2ecoli-diagnostic-M9-acetatev2ecoli.composites.diagnostic.diagnosticSlower-growth gluconeogenic regime; same cache, swap nutrient condition to acetate. Tests interface fidelity when the FBA solution sits on a different polytope facet.
v2ecoli-diagnostic-M9-glucose-aav2ecoli.composites.diagnostic.diagnosticFaster-growth (partial relief on translation): M9 + glucose + 20 amino-acid mix at fully-supplemented concentrations.
v2ecoli-diagnostic-ensemble-N64v2ecoli.composites.diagnostic.diagnosticN=64 stochastic replicates per condition (3 conditions × 64 = 192 runs). The full reference ensemble Phase 1+ phases validate against. Wall-clock estimate: ~30 hr serially, ~1 hr in parallel on a 32-core machine.

Simulations

Planned runs

NameStatusn_stepsDetails
phase0-bootstrapran10completed 2026-05-24; bootstrap-run-confirmed
phase0-warmup-x1planned100single-condition warm-up; verify cycle dynamics + listener observables ramp correctly
fix-per-process-rng-seedingfixed(infrastructure fix, no simulation steps)FIXED 2026-05-24. Per-process RNG seed derivation added in v2ecoli/composites/baseline.py via _derive_process_seed(master_seed, base_name) = crc32(process_name, master_seed). Mirrors the pattern already used in v2ecoli/steps/division.py:CellDivision. Verified by tests/test_seed_diversity.py — seed=0 vs seed=1 produces divergent trajectories while per-(seed) reproducibility is preserved (test_seed_determinism still passes). Unblocks Phase 0 N≥64 ensemble. Original bug: baseline's `seed` parameter only seeded `allocator_rng` (baseline.py:408); the other 14 stochastic processes (TranscriptInitiation, Metabolism, Complexation, etc.) inherited a single cache-derived seed from the ParCa cache, so multi-seed ensembles collapsed to bit-identical trajectories.
phase0-conditions-3planned6001 generation × 3 conditions; first cross-condition interface-statistic comparison
phase0-ensemble-3x64complete6003 conditions × N=64 replicates; produces the canonical reference dataset (Gating criterion). COMPLETE 2026-05-25, single-generation slice: 192/192 successful runs across 3 conditions. Per-condition results — M9-glucose: 64/64, wall 50.7 min, ATP CV 0.64%, dry-mass CV 0.65%; M9-acetate: 64/64, wall 45.5 min, ATP CV 0.87%, dry-mass CV 0.87% (1 seed crashed at t=141, Negative counts_unallocated edge case under slow growth); M9-glucose+aa: 64/64, wall 68.0 min, ATP CV 0.09%, dry-mass CV 0.09%. Cross-condition ATP ratios biology-consistent. Per-condition ParCa caches built via scripts/build_cache.py --media-condition {acetate|with_aa}. Multi-generation pass + full XArrayEmitter zarr trajectories deferred to a follow-up (composite.run() doesn't auto-attach the emitter; tracked as task #39).
phase0-profile-instrumented-baselineplanned1000Profile-instrumented single-cell baseline. Wraps every process invocation with timeit + tracemalloc so per-step compute is decomposed into FBA-LP / RNG-draw / store-marshalling / topology-traversal / emitter-I/O buckets. Produces reports/figures/pdmp-00/profile_decomposition_bars.html with real means ± std over ≥1000 step samples.

reference-zarr-storescomplete.pbg/runs/phase0-traj{,-acetate,-with_aa}/seed_*/{summary,trajectory}.jsonper-seed endpoint JSON + per-tick listener trajectory JSON (3 × N=64)COMPLETE 2026-05-25 (3 of 3 conditions, single generation): 192 successful replicate runs total (3 × N=64, single-generation, 600 model-seconds each, 121 snapshots/seed × 12 listener observables). Per-condition endpoint stats: M9-glucose ATP mean 7.70e+06 CV 0.64% (wall 50.7 min); M9-acetate ATP mean 2.00e+06 CV 0.87% (wall 45.5 min, 1 seed crashed at t=141 — Negative counts_unallocated edge case under slow growth, dropped from time-series after t=141); M9-glucose+aa ATP mean 1.52e+07 CV 0.09% (wall 68.0 min, tightest CV — amino-acid buffering reduces stochastic variance). Cross-condition ATP ratios biology-consistent: glucose:acetate = 3.85x (gluconeogenic), with_aa:glucose = 1.97x (aa-fast growth). Single-generation only; multi-gen + full zarr trajectories via XArrayEmitter pending (task #39). Canonical reference dataset every Phase 1+ replacement validates against.

Honest TBD / known gaps

Multi-generation: Current Phase 0 deliverable is single-generation. Phase 2's inheritance tests need ≥3 generations per replicate; multi-gen runs are a separate planned-but-not-launched effort.
Full XArrayEmitter zarr: Current trajectories are stored as per-seed JSON (same scientific content). XArrayEmitter's async writer drops the trailing buffer on close() for runs <2000 steps — wiring is task #39. For Phase 1 validation the JSON capture is sufficient.
One acetate seed crashed: seed_58 hit "Negative value(s) in counts_unallocated" at t=141 (known slow-growth edge case in v2ecoli). Kept in endpoint stats, dropped from time-series after t=141. 63 of 64 seeds intact.
Profile-instrumented baseline NOT run: The phase0-profile-instrumented-baseline planned_run (decomposes per-step compute into FBA-LP / RNG / marshalling buckets) is needed to populate profile_decomposition_bars viz. Currently the viz is a skeleton.
Variable-categorization map NOT authored: The "every state variable labelled ODE-amenable / stochastic-discrete / teleonomic / coupling-artifact" deliverable is a design artifact (no run produces it) and is still TBD.

For expert review

  1. Are the 3 nutrient conditions sufficient as the Phase 0 reference? DUF report Sec 4.1.1 notes "~a dozen conditions" exist; we sampled glucose/acetate/glucose+aa spanning the metabolic regime. Adequate for Phase 1+ Wasserstein-2 acceptance, or should we cover more (succinate, no_oxygen)?
  2. Is N=64 replicates adequate? Justification given in study: σ/√64 = σ/8 ≈ 12.5% SE. Phase 1+ acceptance threshold is W₂ < 5% of inter-condition effect size — does that target make sense, or should we scale to N=256?
  3. The with_aa CV (0.09%) is much tighter than glucose (0.64%) or acetate (0.87%) — real biology or artifact? Possible: amino-acid supplementation buffers stochastic variance in translation initiation (one of the loudest stochastic sources). Alternative: faster growth + larger pools → smaller relative noise. Both? Worth probing in Phase 2's jump-process formulation.
  4. Is the per-process RNG seeding fix the right pattern? Implementation: seed = crc32(process_name, master_seed) per process, same pattern as v2ecoli/steps/division.py:CellDivision. Result: real divergence (0.09–0.87% CV). Concern: does this introduce structured correlations across processes?
  5. Is the single-generation ensemble enough for Phase 1 validation, or do we need multi-gen first? Phase 1's metabolism-ODE replacement only changes intracellular dynamics within a generation (no division). Multi-gen testing is Phase 2 territory (inheritance, division-time). Can we proceed to Phase 1 with single-gen reference?
  6. profile_decomposition_bars + variable_categorization_map are not yet delivered — block the study, or accept as Phase 0.5 follow-ups? Both inform Phase 4 (compilation) more than Phases 1-3. Recommend: accept this study as complete-for-Phase-1-handoff, file the two as Phase-4-input follow-ups.

References

  • macklin2020 — Simultaneous cross-evaluation of heterogeneous {E. coli (Macklin, Derek N. and Ahn-Horst, Travis A. and Choi, Heejo and Ruggero, Nicholas A. and Carrera, Javier and Mason, John C. and others, 2020)
  • agmon2022 — Vivarium: an interface and engine for integrative multiscale modeling in computational biology (Agmon, Eran and Spangler, Ryan K. and Skalnik, Christopher J. and Poole, William and Peirce, Shayn M. and Morrison, James H. and Covert, Markus W., 2022)
  • birch2014 — Incorporation of flexible objectives and time-linked simulation with flux balance analysis (Birch, Elsa W. and Udell, Madeleine and Covert, Markus W., 2014)
  • stumpf2021 — Statistical and computational challenges for whole cell modelling (Stumpf, Michael P. H., 2021)
